source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/06_Risk Management/23G series/A-TCF-5-105 FMEA Table (rev.2).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: FMEA report for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under Rev. 2 dated March 8, 2024.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk control measures, verification references, and overall residual risk acceptability for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
keyPoints: Essential performance includes intended image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation when required, and normal program operation when connected to power supply.
The detector is designed for an expected service life of approximately 5 years.

Risk controls include labels and user manual information, certified battery use, IEC 60601-1 testing, differentiated cable designs, IEC 62304 software development process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module use.
After implementation and verification of risk control measures, residual risks are stated to be reduced to the acceptable region.

evidence.claim: The document identifies itself as FMEA Report, Doc No. A-TCF-5-105, Revision No. Rev. 2, with revision date March 8, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText:
Doc No. : A-TCF-5-105
Revision date : March 8, 2024
Revision No. : Rev. 2
FMEA Report

evidence.claim: The covered product models are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: The standards listed at the document header are EN ISO 14971:2019, IEC 60601-1:2006/AMD1:2013, EN 62366-1:2015, and Radio Equipment Directive (RED).
evidence.evidenceText: Standard : EN ISO 14971:2019 + IEC 60601-1:2006/AMD1:2013 + EN 62366-1:2015 + Radio Equipment Directive (RED)

evidence.claim: Revision 2 was dated 2024.03.08 and described as a response to the 2nd review by 3EC (NB).

Essential performance includes intended image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank acquired image, normal operation when required, DC power supplied during EMC test, and normal manufacturer program operation when connected to power supply.
evidence.evidenceText:
Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.
- Acquisition image is obtained as intended.
- The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.
- Operation normally when operation is required.
- DC power is consistently supplied from POWER SUPPLY when EMC
test is performed.
- When connected to POWER SUPPLY, program provided by
manufacturer is operated normally.

evidence.claim: The expected service life for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText:
The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life
of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: The device has a rechargeable battery.
evidence.evidenceText:
7.3.3 Batteries A The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW has the rechargeable
battery.

evidence.claim: The device has a Type B applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.5.2.2 Type B applied parts A Type B Applied part

evidence.claim: The device does not have patient leads or patient cables.
evidence.evidenceText:
8.5.2.3 Patient Leads or Patient cables N/A The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW does not have the patient
lead or patient cable.

evidence.claim: The device does not produce diagnostic or therapeutic X-radiation.
evidence.evidenceText:
10.1.2 ME equipment intended to produce diagnostic or therapeutic X-radiation N/A The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW does not produce the x-
radiation for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

evidence.claim: Software risk controls and validation are linked to IEC 62304 compliance.
evidence.evidenceText:
Software risk management process was
established in compliance with IEC 62304 and
software validation was performed. Therefore
there is no unacceptable risk.

evidence.claim: Data transmission is made from the device to the PC for IT-network related PEMS consideration.
evidence.evidenceText: 14.13 PEMS intended to be incorporated into an IT-Network A Data transmission is made from the device to the PC.

evidence.claim: Risk controls include labels and user manual information, certified battery use, IEC 60601-1 testing, differentiated cable designs, IEC 62304 software development process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module use.
evidence.evidenceText:
As control measures for the identified risks,
providing label and user manual, using
certified battery, testing the device to meet
IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type
differently, establishing software development
process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-
related testing, and using certified RF module,
etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: After risk control implementation and verification, residual risks are stated to be acceptable and no new hazards requiring additional controls were identified.
evidence.evidenceText:
After all risk control measures have been
implemented and verified, the residual risks
within the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector are
reduced in the acceptable region, and the
new hazards needed for additional risk control
measures are not identified.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous: the header lists Radio Equipment Directive (RED), and revision history mentions 3EC (NB), but no explicit jurisdiction or certificate authority scope is provided.
OCR/source quality issues are present, including apparent typos such as 'Digital faly panel', 'date cable', 'hazzards', and inconsistent punctuation.
The source metadata productSignal is HnX, but the extracted product models in the text are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
